title: fix: improve seed upgrade scoring and logging
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Better math for Seed upgrades
gist.blurb: We've tuned the GitHub activity scoring for automatic Seed upgrades. The commit window now looks back a full year, and we've squashed the bugs that were getting in the way of legitimate upgrades.
gist.summary: Improved the Spore-to-Seed auto-upgrade script by expanding the commit evaluation window to one year and fixing resource limit errors that caused false rejections.
gist.impact: Users with legitimate GitHub activity will now be reliably and fairly upgraded to the Seed tier without being incorrectly flagged due to API limits.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about upgrading users from Spore (mushroom) to Seed (sprout/plant) based on GitHub activity. Visuals: Polly the bee evaluating a small glowing seed or sprout, holding a clipboard or magnifying glass. A retro computer showing a green checkmark.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny reading glasses and holding a clipboard, carefully inspecting a glowing green seed sprouting in a lime green (#ecf874) flower pot. A retro computer monitor in the background displays a green checkmark and chunky code. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
